Notes.NoteText: Transactions between zero and +/- $500,000.
Suppressed to avoid the disclosure of data of individual companies.
Not applicable
Transactions are possible but are zero for a given period.
Courier services are included in "Air transport, freight" (lines 10 and 113 in tables 2.1 and 2.3) but are not separately identifiable.

Insurance services transactions are considered to be unaffiliated even when they are between affiliated companies because the services are considered to be provided to the policyholders who pay the insurance premiums and who are unaffiliated with either company. The only insurance services considered to be affiliated are primary insurance transactions between a U.S. company that is not an insurance company and an affiliated foreign insurance company, such as a captive foreign insurance affiliate. Data on these affiliated insurance services are not separately available.
Outcomes of research and development include patents, industrial processes, and trade secrets.

This category includes installation, alteration, and training services; contract manufacturing services; photographic services (including satellite photography services); and other business services n.i.e.

Insurance services transactions are considered to be unaffiliated even when they are between affiliated companies because the services are considered to be provided to the policyholders who pay the insurance premiums and who are unaffiliated with either company. The only insurance services considered to be affiliated are primary insurance transactions between a U.S. company that is not an insurance company and an affiliated foreign insurance company, such as a captive foreign insurance affiliate. This category includes installation, alteration, and training services; contract manufacturing services; photographic services (including satellite photography services); and other business services n.i.e.

For the years 1999-2005, these values are estimated using the methods described in the box "Defining ICT and Potentially ICT-Enabled Trade in Services," which appears in Alexis Grimm, "Trends in U.S. Trade in Information and Communications Technology (ICT) Services and in ICT-Enabled Services" SURVEY OF CURRENT BUSINESS 96 (May 2016).

For the years 1999-2005, these values are estimated using the methods described in the box "Defining ICT and Potentially ICT-Enabled Trade in Services," which appears in Alexis Grimm, "Trends in U.S. Trade in Information and Communications Technology (ICT) Services and in ICT-Enabled Services" SURVEY OF CURRENT BUSINESS 96 (May 2016).

Insurance services transactions are considered to be unaffiliated even when they are between affiliated companies because the services are considered to be provided to the policyholders who pay the insurance premiums and who are unaffiliated with either company. The only insurance services considered to be affiliated are primary insurance transactions between a U.S. company that is not an insurance company and an affiliated foreign insurance company, such as a captive foreign insurance affiliate. Data on these affiliated insurance services are not separately available.
